overview: Cagliari, the vibrant capital of Sardinia, Italy, is strategically located on the southern coast of the island. Known for its rich history, stunning architecture, and beautiful beaches, Cagliari serves as a cultural and economic hub in the Mediterranean. With a population of approximately 149,257, it plays a vital role in connecting Sardinia to mainland Italy and beyond, making it an essential destination for tourists and business travelers alike.
history: Cagliari has ancient roots, having been founded by the Phoenicians around the 8th century BC. Throughout history, it has served as a critical port and military stronghold, reflecting various cultural influences.
economy: Cagliari's economy is diverse, with key industries including tourism, agriculture, and shipping. The city is a central business hub for the region, fostering trade and commerce while attracting investments in various sectors.
tourism: Tourism in Cagliari thrives on its stunning coastal scenery and historical landmarks. Visitors flock to the ancient Castello district, relax on the beautiful Poetto beach, and explore the Roman amphitheater, all while enjoying the city's rich culinary offerings and vibrant nightlife.

best_restaurants.name: Ristorante Sa Piola
best_restaurants.description: A charming eatery known for its traditional Sardinian cuisine and warm atmosphere.

best_restaurants.name: Trattoria Lillicu
best_restaurants.description: Famous for its fresh seafood dishes and local flavors, this restaurant is a favorite among locals.

best_restaurants.name: Il Ristorante di Mare
best_restaurants.description: Offers a stunning view of the sea along with a menu focused on Mediterranean seafood.

best_restaurants.name: Su Cumbidu
best_restaurants.description: Known for its rustic decor and hearty Sardinian dishes, providing a true taste of the island.

best_restaurants.name: Pizzeria Da Marco
best_restaurants.description: A popular spot for delicious, wood-fired pizzas with a variety of toppings.

weather_summary: Cagliari enjoys a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters.

faqs.question: What time zone is Cagliari in?
faqs.answer: Cagliari is in the Europe/Rome time zone.

faqs.question: Does Cagliari observe daylight saving time?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cagliari observes daylight saving time, typically from the last Sunday in March to the last Sunday in October.

faqs.question: What is the population of Cagliari?
faqs.answer: Cagliari has a population of approximately 149,257 residents.

faqs.question: When does sunrise typically occur in Cagliari?
faqs.answer: Sunrise in Cagliari typically occurs around 6:30 AM in summer and about 7:30 AM in winter.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Cagliari?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Cagliari is during the spring and early fall when the weather is pleasant and tourist crowds are smaller.
